title: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (approves/enforces NERC standards)
Basic Insulation Level (lightning impulse withstand rating)
Facilities Design, Connections, and Maintenance (NERC standard category)
Critical Infrastructure Protection (NERC cyber/physical security category)
Voltage and Reactive Power Control (NERC standard category)
Modeling, Data, and Analysis (NERC standard category)
Personnel Performance, Training, and Qualifications (NERC standard category)
Underfrequency Load Shedding / Undervoltage Load Shedding
Generic Object Oriented Substation Event / Manufacturing Message Specification (IEC 61850 messaging types)
OSHA standard for electric power generation, transmission, and distribution work practices (pairs closely with NESC Work Rules)
Current Transformer / Potential (Voltage) Transformer - instrument transformers for metering & protection
Valve-Regulated Lead-Acid (sealed battery technology, see IEEE 1187)
National Electrical Code (NFPA 70) - distinct from the NESC, generally not applicable to utility supply equipment
Guide for Safety in AC Substation Grounding
detail: The bible for grounding grid design - step/touch voltage, ground grid resistance. Every substation grounding design gets checked against this.

title: Standard for Qualifying Permanent Connections Used in Substation Grounding
detail: Governs the ground connections/exothermic welds and compression fittings used in the grounding grid.

title: Guide for Performing Arc-Flash Hazard Calculations
detail: Used to calculate arc-flash incident energy for labeling and PPE requirements - directly affects site safety program.

title: Guide for Recommended Electrical Clearances and Insulation Levels in Air-Insulated Substations
detail: Sets minimum clearances between energized parts and structures - directly affects site layout and BIL selection.

title: Electric Power Systems and Equipment - Voltage Ratings (60 Hz)
detail: Standard nominal system voltages and tolerances - referenced any time voltage class is specified.

title: Guide for Design, Construction, and Operation of Electric Power Substations for Community Acceptance and Environmental Compatibility
detail: Community/environmental siting considerations - noise, visual, EMF concerns during permitting.

title: Recommended Practice for Seismic Design of Substations
detail: Required in seismic zones - equipment anchoring, structural qualification testing.

title: Guide for Substation Fire Protection
detail: Fire protection guidance specific to substations - spacing, fire barriers, suppression system selection. Read alongside NFPA 850.

title: General Requirements for Liquid-Immersed Distribution, Power, and Regulating Transformers
detail: Baseline spec transformers are built and tested to - referenced constantly in procurement and factory testing.

title: Standard Requirements for Liquid-Immersed Power Transformers
detail: Governs larger power transformers (750 kVA three-phase and up) typically used in substations, vs. smaller distribution units.

title: Test Code for Liquid-Immersed Distribution, Power, and Regulating Transformers
detail: Governs factory acceptance testing procedures for transformers - relevant when you witness FAT.

title: Standard Requirements for Liquid-Immersed Distribution Substation Transformers
detail: Covers the small/mid power transformer class often used at distribution substations.

title: Standard Requirements for Instrument Transformers
detail: Governs CTs and PTs (current/potential transformers) used for metering and protection - referenced on nearly every relay/metering scheme.

title: General Requirements & Application Guide for Power Apparatus Bushings
detail: Bushing design/application requirements and guidance - insulation failure prevention.

title: Guide for Transformer Impulse Tests
detail: Governs how BIL/impulse withstand testing is performed and interpreted on transformers.

title: Guide for Transformer Condition Assessment
detail: Used for evaluating existing/aging transformers - relevant on retrofit or expansion projects.

title: Guide for the Interpretation of Gases Generated in Oil-Immersed Transformers
detail: Interpreting Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) results - core diagnostic tool for transformer health.

title: Standard Rating Structure for AC High-Voltage Circuit Breakers
detail: Defines the rating structure (interrupting rating, voltage class, etc.) breakers are specified against.

title: Preferred Ratings and Related Required Capabilities for AC High-Voltage Circuit Breakers
detail: Standard ratings table for breakers - used when reviewing equipment specs/submittals.

title: Test Procedures for AC High-Voltage Circuit Breakers
detail: Governs factory and field testing procedures for circuit breakers.

title: Standard Requirements for AC High-Voltage Air Switches Rated Above 1000 V
detail: The consolidated modern standard for disconnect/grounding/interrupter switches (supersedes the older C37.30-C37.37 series).

title: Standard for Metal-Clad Switchgear
detail: Governs metal-clad switchgear construction - relevant for GIS/AIS equipment specs.

title: Standard for High-Voltage Gas-Insulated Substations, Rated Above 52 kV
detail: Core standard if the project is a GIS (gas-insulated) substation rather than air-insulated.

title: Standard for Metal-Oxide Surge Arresters for AC Power Circuits (>1 kV)
detail: The primary surge arrester product standard - referenced on every arrester spec/submittal.

title: Guide for the Application of Metal-Oxide Surge Arresters for AC Systems
detail: Surge arrester selection/application guidance to protect equipment from lightning/switching surges.

title: Guide for the Application of Neutral Grounding in Electrical Utility Systems
detail: Governs grounding/neutral scheme selection (solidly grounded, resistance grounded, etc.) - affects fault current and equipment ratings.

title: Standard for Relays and Relay Systems Associated with Electric Power Apparatus
detail: Governs protective relay design/testing requirements - core to protection engineering coordination.

title: Standard Surge Withstand Capability Tests for Relays and Relay Systems
detail: Tests relay immunity to electrical noise/surges - relevant to control house wiring and shielding practices.

title: Standard Electrical Power System Device Function Numbers
detail: The numbering system (e.g., 87 = differential, 50/51 = overcurrent) used on every one-line diagram and relay setting sheet. Learn this early - it's how the whole industry talks.

title: Standard for SCADA and Automation Systems
detail: Governs SCADA/automation architecture for substation control systems.

title: Guide for the Application of Current Transformers Used for Protective Relaying Purposes
detail: Sizing/application of CTs specifically for protection accuracy - relevant to relay coordination studies.

title: Guide for Power System Protection Testing
detail: Guide for testing protection systems - relevant to commissioning/acceptance test plans.

title: Recommended Practice for Maintenance, Testing, and Replacement of Vented Lead-Acid Batteries for Stationary Applications
detail: Governs ongoing maintenance/testing of the station battery bank that powers protection/control during an outage.

title: Recommended Practice for Installation Design and Installation of Vented Lead-Acid Batteries for Stationary Applications
detail: Battery room design - ventilation, spacing, seismic bracing, safety.

title: Recommended Practice for Sizing Vented Lead-Acid Batteries for Stationary Applications
detail: Sizing calculation method for the DC battery bank based on load/duty cycle.

title: Recommended Practice for Installation Design and Installation of Valve-Regulated Lead-Acid Batteries for Stationary Applications
detail: Same purpose as IEEE 484 but for VRLA (sealed) battery technology, increasingly common in new builds.

title: Recommended Practice for Installation, Maintenance, Testing, and Replacement of Vented Nickel-Cadmium Batteries for Stationary Applications
detail: Same purpose as 450/484 but for NiCad battery technology, sometimes used in extreme temperature sites.

title: Guide for Bus Design in Air Insulated Substations
detail: Bus structure design (rigid/strain bus) - relevant to structural/electrical layout review.

title: Design Guide for Substation Structures (ASCE Manual 74 family)
detail: Structural design of steel/dead-end structures - referenced alongside ASCE 10 (lattice towers) if applicable.

title: Standard Environmental and Testing Requirements for Communications Networking Devices in Electric Power Substations
detail: Environmental/EMI testing requirements for network switches, RTUs, etc. installed in the substation.

title: Standard for Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) Cyber Security Capabilities
detail: Cybersecurity requirements for relays/IEDs - ties directly into NERC CIP compliance.

title: Communication Networks and Systems for Power Utility Automation (adopted alongside IEEE)
detail: The modern digital substation communication standard (GOOSE, MMS, SCL). Increasingly the default for new builds.

title: Standard for SCADA and Automation Systems (see also Protection & Relaying)
detail: Cross-referenced here since it also governs automation database/architecture requirements.

title: Protection System, Automatic Reclosing, and Sudden Pressure Relaying Maintenance
detail: Sets mandatory maintenance/testing intervals for relays, batteries, breakers - directly shapes your commissioning and maintenance handoff requirements.

title: Protection System Misoperation Identification and Correction
detail: Requires investigation/correction of relay misoperations - relevant post-commissioning.

title: Transmission Relay Loadability
detail: Ensures protective relay settings don't trip on normal loading - relevant to protection coordination studies.

title: Coordination of Protection Systems for Performance During Faults
detail: Requires coordination/documentation of protection settings across interconnected systems.

title: Disturbance Monitoring and Reporting Requirements
detail: Requires adequate disturbance monitoring equipment/data for post-event analysis.

title: Disturbance Monitoring Equipment Installation and Data Reporting
detail: Governs disturbance recorders/DFR equipment often specified in substation design.

title: Coordination of Generating Unit or Plant Capabilities, Voltage Regulating Controls, and Protection
detail: Relevant if the substation interconnects to a generating plant/synchronous condenser.

title: Frequency and Voltage Protection Settings for Generating Resources
detail: Relevant on generation-interconnection substations - ride-through settings coordination.

title: Facility Ratings
detail: Requires documented methodology for equipment ratings (transformers, breakers, bus) - directly ties into equipment nameplate/rating verification during procurement.

title: Facility Interconnection Requirements
detail: Sets the requirements a Transmission Owner must publish for new facilities connecting to its system - relevant during interconnection studies.

title: Facility Interconnection Studies
detail: Requires interconnection studies be performed for new/modified facilities - relevant during project planning phase.

title: Transmission Vegetation Management
detail: Mostly line-focused, but relevant if the substation site has ROW/vegetation clearance requirements.

title: Verification and Data Reporting of Generator Real and Reactive Power Capability
detail: Relevant on generation-interconnected substations - capability verification testing.

title: Verification of Models and Data for Generator Excitation/Governor/Dynamic Response
detail: Relevant on generation-interconnected substations for dynamic model validation.

title: Physical Security
detail: Identifies critical transmission substations requiring enhanced physical security (following the 2013 Metcalf substation attack) - directly affects fencing, cameras, intrusion detection design on critical sites.

title: BES Cyber System Categorization
detail: The starting point of the CIP suite - determines which of your substation's systems are even in scope for the rest of CIP.

title: Electronic Security Perimeter(s)
detail: Governs network security boundaries around substation control/protection systems.

title: Physical Security of BES Cyber Systems
detail: Governs physical access controls specifically for cyber system equipment (separate from CIP-014's broader site security).

title: Systems Security Management, Incident Reporting, Recovery Plans, Configuration Change Management, Vendor Risk Management
detail: Rest of the cyber security lifecycle suite - relevant once the substation's control systems are classified as BES Cyber Systems.

title: Generator Operation for Maintaining Network Voltage Schedules
detail: Relevant if the substation includes generation interconnection or reactive power/voltage support equipment.

title: Operating Personnel Communications Protocols
detail: Standardized communication protocols for operating instructions - relevant once the substation is operational and staffed/remotely operated.

title: Transmission Operations / Interconnection Reliability Operations
detail: Governs real-time operational coordination - relevant once the substation is energized and operating, less so during construction.

title: Systematic Approach to Training
detail: Requires personnel performing real-time operations be trained systematically - relevant to your commissioning/handover training plan for operators.

title: System Restoration from Blackstart Resources
detail: Relevant if the substation plays a role in a blackstart/restoration plan.

code: Part 1 - Rules for Electric Supply Stations
title: Part 1 - Rules for Electric Supply Stations
detail:
Substations, generating plants, and other electric supply installations

This is THE part of the NESC that directly governs substations (as opposed to overhead/underground lines).

Notes: Start here - it's your home section

title: Section 11 - Purpose, Scope, Application
detail:
General applicability and definitions

Clarifies what counts as a "supply station" and when the code applies.

title: Section 12 - General Requirements
detail:
General safety requirements for supply stations

Baseline safety expectations for the whole facility.

title: Section 13 - Enclosures
detail:
Fencing, locks, warning signs for restricted access

Directly relevant to site security/fencing design - a routine owner's rep review item.

Notes: Ties into NERC CIP-014 physical security too

title: Section 14 - Working Space and Clearances
detail:
Minimum working space around equipment, aisle clearances

You'll reference this when reviewing equipment layout drawings for maintenance access.

title: Section 15 - Illumination
detail:
Lighting requirements

Site lighting design review item.

title: Section 16 - Guarding of Live Parts
detail:
Barriers/guarding for exposed energized equipment

Directly affects fencing, barriers, and equipment enclosure design.

title: Section 17 - General Protection Requirements
detail:
Protective grounding, fire protection, drainage

Grounding, oil containment, and fire protection systems all trace back here.

title: Section 18 - Rotating Equipment
detail:
Generators/motors at supply stations

Usually less relevant to pure substations unless there's on-site generation.

Notes: Lower priority for pure substation work

title: Section 19 - Storage Batteries
detail:
DC battery systems for protection/control power

Battery room design, ventilation, spacing - relevant since every substation has a DC system for protection.

Notes: Cross-reference IEEE 450/484/1187

title: Sections 20-29 - Overhead Lines Rules
detail:
Clearances, strength/loading (Grade B/C construction), grounding for lines entering/leaving the substation

Relevant at the substation's dead-end structures where transmission lines terminate.

Notes: Grade B = higher strength, typically required for transmission

title: Sections 30-39 - Underground Lines Rules
detail:
Underground cable/duct requirements

Relevant if the substation has underground cable getaways.

title: Sections 40-44 - Work Rules
detail:
Safe work practices for utility workers (energized/de-energized work, PPE)

Governs how your contractors' crews are required to work safely on site - overlaps with OSHA 1910.269.

Notes: Cross-reference with OSHA 1910.269

title: Electric Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution
detail: The primary general-industry standard for O&M work on substations - qualified-person rules, minimum approach distances, PPE, grounding, and job briefings.

title: Electric Power Transmission and Distribution (Construction)
detail: Construction-phase companion to 1910.269 - governs new substation builds, alterations, and improvements before the facility is in service.

title: General Requirements (Electrical, Construction)
detail: Working clearances, guarding of live parts, and access-control rules (locks, warning signs) for equipment over 600V - directly relevant to substation layout and fencing review.

title: Electrical (Construction Wiring Methods)
detail: Governs wiring methods/components for construction electrical work generally, including control house and low-voltage wiring during the build.

title: General Requirements (PPE)
detail: Requires the hazard assessment that determines what PPE is needed in the first place - the foundation document behind every PPE decision.

title: Eye and Face Protection
detail: Governs safety glasses/face shields required for general site work and arc-flash exposure.

title: Head Protection
detail: Hard hat requirements for the site.

title: Foot Protection
detail: Safety footwear requirements for the site.

title: Electrical Protective Equipment
detail: Governs insulating rubber gloves, sleeves, and blankets - including required periodic dielectric testing intervals.

title: Hand Protection
detail: General hand protection requirements beyond electrical rubber gloves (cut/chemical resistant gloves, etc.).

title: Control of Hazardous Energy (Lockout/Tagout)
detail: General-industry LOTO baseline. Note: for power generation, transmission, and distribution equipment, 1910.269(d)/(m) provides the specific LOTO requirements that apply instead of 1910.147's general provisions.

title: Lockout and Tagging of Circuits (Construction)
detail: Construction-phase lockout/tagging requirement for circuits and equipment being worked on.

title: Walking-Working Surfaces (incl. 1910.28 / 1910.29)
detail: General industry fall protection baseline referenced by 1910.269(g) for substation structure/equipment work.

title: Fall Protection (Construction)
detail: Governs fall protection during the construction phase - relevant to steel erection, structure climbing, and bus work before energization.

title: Permit-Required Confined Spaces
detail: Applies to cable vaults, manholes, and below-grade duct/trench work at the substation.

title: Confined Spaces in Construction
detail: Construction-phase confined space entry requirements - relevant to foundation, grounding trench, and vault work during the build.

title: Excavations
detail: Governs trenching/excavation safety for grounding grids, duct banks, and foundations - a routine owner's rep inspection item.

title: Scaffolds
detail: Scaffold safety requirements for structure/bus work during construction.

title: Cranes and Derricks in Construction
detail: Governs rigging/lifting operations - directly relevant to setting transformers, breakers, and steel structures.

title: Hazard Communication
detail: Governs labeling/SDS requirements for transformer oil, SF6 decomposition byproducts, and (on older equipment) PCB-containing oil.

title: Respiratory Protection
detail: Relevant for SF6 handling/decomposition byproduct exposure and confined space entry.

title: Recording and Reporting Occupational Injuries and Illnesses
detail: Governs your OSHA 300 log and incident recordkeeping obligations for the project.

title: General Duty Clause
detail: Catch-all requirement to address recognized hazards even where no specific OSHA standard exists - commonly cited when a hazard falls outside an existing rule's literal scope.

category: OSHA eTool: Electric Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution
title: Open eTool
Recommended Practice for Fire Protection for Electric Generating Plants and HVDC Converter Stations
detail: Companion to IEEE 979 - governs transformer spacing, fire barriers, and suppression system design for substations/generating stations.

title: Standard for Water Spray Fixed Systems for Fire Protection
detail: Governs design/testing of water spray fire suppression systems sometimes used on large oil-filled transformers.

title: National Electrical Code (NEC)
detail: Generally does not apply to utility-owned supply/transmission/distribution equipment, but does apply to station-service/control-house wiring in many jurisdictions. Know the utility exclusion.

title: Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace
detail: Governs arc-flash PPE categories and safe work practices for personnel - used alongside IEEE 1584 arc-flash calculations.

title: Design of Latticed Steel Transmission Structures
detail: Governs lattice tower structural design - relevant if the substation's dead-end/takeoff structures use lattice construction.

title: Design of Steel Transmission Pole Structures
detail: Governs steel pole structural design - the tubular-pole alternative to lattice construction.

title: Substation Structure Design Guide
detail: General guide for substation structural design - cross-referenced with IEEE/ASCE 113 in the IEEE tab.

title: Electric Power Systems and Equipment - Voltage Ratings (60 Hz)
detail: Defines standard nominal voltage classes and acceptable ranges - baseline reference for any voltage-related spec.

title: Electric Power Connection for Substations
detail: Covers connector hardware standards sometimes referenced alongside IEEE 837 for grounding hardware.
